title: AB 1649 part of Assemblymember Muratsuchi’s “California Refinery Jobs and Safety Action Plan,” which includes five Assembly bills to improve public safety at all California refiner
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: AB 1649 will codify and make permanent the existing Interagency Refinery Task Force.
The bill is part of the California Refinery Jobs and Safety Action Plan which includes five Assembly bills to improve public safety at all California refineries.
AB 1645 will phase out the use of hydrofluoric acid by California refineries.
AB 1646 will require the maintenance of a publicly accessible community alert plan for emergencies.
AB 1647 will require refineries to install and maintain air quality monitors at the refinery fence line and in the community.
The governing air quality agency will be required to publicly report real-time readings from the air quality monitors.
AB 1648 will increase the number of state refinery inspectors at the California Division of Occupational Safety and Health (Cal/OSHA).
